focus_areas: predicate reordering: apply most-selective predicates first (l_discount BETWEEN 0.09 → l_quantity < 0.48 → l_shipdate ranges 0.145 each)
SIMD-vectorized filtering for all 4 predicates in single pass: avoid repeated full-table scans
branch-free filtering or bit-mask filtering to minimize branch misprediction
single aggregate SUM() — no partial aggregation needed (no GROUP BY, just one scalar output)
utilize l_shipdate_sorted index and zone maps to skip blocks outside date range before applying other predicates
vectorized arithmetic for revenue computation (l_extendedprice * l_discount) on filtered results
strategy_notes: Q6 is a simple single-table scan with 4 independent filters and one scalar aggregate. Key is predicate evaluation efficiency. Load l_shipdate column, apply zone map to skip blocks outside [1994-01-01, 1995-01-01). Then scan remaining blocks, apply l_discount BETWEEN (most selective 0.09), then l_quantity < 24, then remaining date filter. Use SIMD to process 4-8 rows per instruction. Single final SUM() — no hash aggregation needed. Expected: 1.3-1.5x speedup to reach DuckDB ~20ms.
reasoning: GenDB shows strong performance on Q1 (1.8-3.2x faster than DuckDB) but critical underperformance on Q3 (3.4-5x slower) and Q6 (1.4-1.5x slower). The 64-core, 376GB hardware enables aggressive parallelism. Priority: (1) fix Q3 join strategy and selectivity pushdown, (2) optimize Q6 predicate evaluation order, (3) maintain Q1's strong scaling through morsel-driven parallelism and SIMD.
